05ddc076 canonical: chemostat (concept) — flat · 1 aliases · 1 embeddings
aliases (1): chemostat
embeddings (1): chemostat
5 fact sample(s)
  • Continuous (chemostat) cultures in bioreactor vessels are applied as an alternative to serial batch growth, as cited in references [45–50].
  • Chemostat cultivation provides the advantages of constant growth rates and population densities, as well as the ability to tightly control nutrient supply and environmental conditions like pH and oxygenation.
  • Chemostat cultivation has a major drawback in its high operational costs, which significantly exceed the costs of shake flask cultivations, even when using small vessels and parallel operation.
  • A primary difference between prolonged selective growth in batch cultures and chemostat cultures is the distinction between nutrient-sufficient conditions and potentially nutrient-limited conditions.
  • In a 1997 study, Escherichia coli was evolved in chemostat cultures to select for improved growth on glycerol, resulting in phenotypic changes including altered colony morphology, increased growth rate, increased biomass yield, and decreased acetate formation.

  • Orange is developing a 'dynamic environment' concept that mixes individual posts for sedentary employees with shared posts subdivided into individual spaces for concentration and group spaces for meetings, allocated by activity and time of day.
  • In the 'dynamic environment' configuration developed by Orange, the plan provides for 1.5 work positions per employee when accounting for all available spaces.

5 fact sample(s)
  • In experiments conducted on the International Space Station (ISS), Deinococcus radiodurans survived for three years outside the station in a shielded compartment, establishing the organism as a relevant model for planetary protection and panspermia.
  • Fred Hoyle (1915–2001) was an astronomer, science fiction writer, and panspermia proponent who considered alternatives to carbon-water biochemistry.
  • The study of prebiotic chemistry is organized into three main axes of debate: endogenous models (such as Miller-Urey experiments, hydrothermal sources, and coacervates), exogenous models (such as panspermia and organic molecules in meteorites like Murchison),
  • The theory of panspermia, which suggests life on Earth was seeded by extraterrestrials, is philosophically untenable because it conflicts with divine revelation and fails to address the 'first cause' argument regarding the origin of the extraterrestrials.
  • The scientific community debates whether life arises spontaneously when conditions are right or whether it is transmitted through space.

7ed3df0a canonical: Norman Horowitz (entity) — flat · 1 aliases · 1 embeddings
aliases (1): Norman Horowitz
embeddings (1): Norman Horowitz
9 fact sample(s)
  • Norman Horowitz devised the experiments to detect life on Mars that were conducted by the Viking Lander in 1976.
  • Norman Horowitz argued that the versatility of the carbon atom makes it the most likely element to provide solutions for survival on other planets.
  • Norman Horowitz (1915–2005) was a Caltech geneticist who devised the first experiments to detect life on Mars and considered alternatives to carbon-water biochemistry.
  • Norman Horowitz devised the experiments to detect life on Mars that were carried out by the 1976 Viking Lander, which was the first U.S. mission to successfully land a probe on the surface of Mars.
  • Norman Horowitz argued that the versatility of the carbon atom makes it the element most likely to provide solutions for survival on other planets.
7fe6213d canonical: acidophilic organisms (concept) — flat · 2 aliases · 2 embeddings
aliases (2): acidophilic organisms acidophiles
embeddings (2): acidophilic organisms acidophiles
3 fact sample(s)
  • Acidophilic organisms, or acidophiles, thrive in acidic environments, including conditions with a pH as low as 0, which is comparable to the acidity of battery acid.
  • Acidophilic organisms maintain an internal pH of 7 (neutral) to survive in acidic environments that would otherwise break down their cell walls and membranes.
  • Bioleaching is a method of mining and extracting valuable metals from naturally occurring rock using acidophilic organisms, which also allows for the cleanup of heavy metal waste.
78f72297 canonical: CRISPR-Cas systems (concept) — flat · 2 aliases · 2 embeddings
aliases (2): CRISPR-Cas systems CRISPR/Cas systems
embeddings (2): CRISPR-Cas systems CRISPR/Cas systems
4 fact sample(s)
  • CRISPR-Cas systems derived from the bacterium Streptococcus thermophilus and Taq polymerase derived from the bacterium Thermus aquaticus are among the most successful biotechnological tools derived from extremophiles.
  • Engineered variants of nucleases from Pyrococcus or Thermus species improve the precision and efficiency of CRISPR-Cas systems.
  • CRISPR-Cas systems enable the precise manipulation of stress-response and DNA repair pathways, allowing for the integration of genes that provide radiation resistance, oxidative stress mitigation, and desiccation tolerance.
  • Cong et al. (2013) demonstrated multiplex genome engineering using CRISPR/Cas systems.

  • Hydrogen sulfide is a chemical analog of water, though it is less polar and a weaker inorganic solvent.

  • Tang, T. C. et al. developed biocomposite thermoplastic polyurethanes that incorporate evolved bacterial spores as living fillers to facilitate the disintegration of the polymer.

  • Wang and Zhang recommend the use of genomic barcodes or watermarks as complementary strategies to biocontainment for tracking engineered biological assets.
  • The European Food Safety Authority (EFSA) scientific committee has recommended that engineered microbes be barcoded.
  • In the absence of genomic barcodes, tracking engineered biological assets requires full genome sequencing and analysis using machine learning tools trained on proprietary data sets.
  • The authors of the article argue that physically linking engineered strains to digital twins via genomic barcodes would enhance transparency, collaboration, and traceability of biological assets, while facilitating regulatory approvals.
  • A pragmatic approach to biocontainment involves using genomic barcodes, DNA-based error-correcting codes, and nucleic acids-focused cryptography to provide assurance to scientists, industry, regulators, and the public.

  • The Macuxi people initially attempted to peacefully coexist and work with miners at Mount Roraima, but this effort failed.

  • Businesses using qualitative research software other than Thematic may need to perform manual checks to verify the basis of the information provided.
  • Thematic is a qualitative data analysis software that automates the process of thematic analysis, including initial coding and theme identification, while maintaining research rigor.
  • Thematic provides transparent analysis by allowing every identified theme to be traced back to the original feedback, distinguishing it from black-box AI tools.
  • The Themes Editor in the Thematic software allows researchers to maintain human-in-the-loop control by editing, merging, and validating themes to ensure accuracy.

  • Traditional hypnosis, Ericksonian hypnosis, and self-hypnosis utilize hypnotic induction methods such as visualization or counting backward to induce a hypnotic state.
  • Ericksonian Hypnosis, named after Milton Erickson, utilizes metaphors, conversational hypnosis, and indirect suggestions rather than direct commands.
  • The theory behind Ericksonian Hypnosis involves applying indirect suggestions to the unconscious mind.
  • Ericksonian Hypnosis is considered particularly useful for individuals who are skeptical of traditional hypnosis.
  • The arm levitation technique is a classic Ericksonian hypnosis method where a person closes their eyes, and through descriptive words and communication, sensations are felt and explained to induce a strong hypnotic state useful for pain management.

  • Priority debts are defined as debts with the most serious consequences for non-payment, such as the loss of essential services like domestic utilities or court action that could lead to the loss of liberty, and these must be paid back before all other debts.
  • Vulnerable groups are more likely to have arrears on priority debts, such as domestic bills, which carry the most severe legal consequences.
  • Among respondents with mental health problems in the 2000 UK government survey, the most common arrears were priority debts, including domestic bills, rent, and local authority taxes.
  • Priority debts are defined as debts that can lead to immediate harm if left unpaid, such as eviction or repossession, and include mortgage, rent, utilities, car payments, taxes, and child support.

  • Governor Stephen Hopkins, a signer of the Declaration of Independence and the first Chancellor of the College of Rhode Island, is depicted in portraits with the College Edifice in the background.
  • Rhode Island drafted the first formal protest against new British duties on sugar and molasses, known as the “Remonstrance,” which was personally delivered to London by Governor Stephen Hopkins.
  • Stephen Hopkins wrote in his 1765 pamphlet, 'The Rights of Colonies Examined', that 'Liberty is the greatest blessing that men enjoy, and slavery is the heaviest curse that human nature is capable of,' and that those governed at the will of another without con
  • Stephen Hopkins, who was a slaveowner, argued in 'The Rights of Colonies Examined' that a new tax was an attempt to reduce colonists to slavery, seeing no irony in his position.
  • The Brown brothers sent a copy of Stephen Hopkins' pamphlet 'The Rights of Colonies Examined' to Esek Hopkins, who was aboard the slave ship Sally on the coast of Africa.

  • The ship Mary transported enslaved Africans to the West Indies and returned to Rhode Island with several enslaved people for the Brown family's personal use.
  • Obadiah Brown served as the supercargo, the officer in charge of buying and selling captives, on the 1736 voyage of the ship Mary.

  • The 'bohemian' role at music festivals is curated by marketing teams, brands, and commercial sponsors, rather than being created solely by the individual's presentation of self.

  • Geert Hofstede’s cultural dimensions theory was expanded to include the fifth dimension of Long-Term Orientation versus Short-Term Orientation following his examination of Chinese values.

  • Common mistakes in personal insurance management include underinsuring critical assets, failing to update policies after major life changes, choosing policies based solely on price, and neglecting to understand policy details and limitations.

  • The ability of registered investment advisors to include insurance as a core offering has become more viable due to innovations in fee-only solutions, the removal of direct commissions from transactions, and the integration of insurance platforms into register
  • Registered Investment Advisors (RIAs) typically emphasize investment-centric approaches that rely on earning the risk premium from the stock market to support a retired client's financial goals, according to the whitepaper 'Annuities as an Asset Class for Fee
d8112faf canonical: CLIMB study (concept) — flat · 1 aliases · 1 embeddings
aliases (1): CLIMB study
embeddings (1): CLIMB study
10 fact sample(s)
  • The CLIMB study is a nationally representative longitudinal survey of U.S. adults aged 18 and older, conducted via the AmeriSpeak panel, which is a probability-based sample of U.S. households randomly selected from the NORC National Frame.
  • The CLIMB study collected data across four survey waves during the COVID-19 pandemic: Wave 1 (March 2020 – April 2020), Wave 2 (March 2021 – April 2021), Wave 3 (March 2022 – April 2022), and Wave 4 (March 2023 – April 2023).
  • The analytic sample for the CLIMB study consisted of 1,297 participants who responded to Wave 1 and at least one additional survey wave.
  • The CLIMB study defined the co-occurrence of elevated symptoms of depression and anxiety as a positive screen for both depression (PHQ-9 ≥ 10) and anxiety (GAD-7 ≥ 10) within the same person.
  • The CLIMB study categorized total household debt into three levels: no debt, $1–$19,999, and $20,000 or more.
699a8715 canonical: payment plan (concept) — flat · 1 aliases · 1 embeddings
aliases (1): payment plan
embeddings (1): payment plan
9 fact sample(s)
  • Individuals who are behind on bills should contact creditors before a debt collector becomes involved to attempt to negotiate a new, manageable payment plan.
  • Creditors may be willing to negotiate payment plans or accept less than the total amount owed when contacted by a debtor.
  • When an individual cannot pay debt as planned due to income loss or unexpected expenses, they may be able to contact the creditor directly to arrange a payment plan.
  • A payment plan arranged with a creditor typically involves extending the repayment period, lowering monthly payments, and adding interest to the total amount due.
  • Medical providers often establish payment plans with patients to extend payment terms because it is less costly than sending accounts to debt collection agencies, which charge fees for recovered money.
e58ee55e canonical: student loan deferment (concept) — flat · 1 aliases · 1 embeddings
aliases (1): student loan deferment
embeddings (1): student loan deferment
3 fact sample(s)
  • Student loan deferment is a pause on student loan payments where interest often continues to accrue and stack onto the existing balance.
  • Eligibility for student loan deferment typically includes individuals who are currently in school, unemployed, serving in the military, receiving cancer treatments, or experiencing provable financial difficulties.
  • Student loan deferment or forbearance can create larger long-term financial problems because interest can continue to accrue while payments are paused.

  • To build credit using a secured card, individuals should research cards with no or low annual fees, use the card for small, recurring expenses that can be paid in full each month, and always pay the statement balance by the due date to avoid interest and negat
  • Full-time students can apply for student credit cards with no annual fees, provided they meet income requirements and limit usage to intentional expenses like textbooks.
978fe0d2 canonical: wash sales (concept) — flat · 1 aliases · 1 embeddings
aliases (1): wash sales
embeddings (1): wash sales
8 fact sample(s)
  • Investors utilizing tax-loss harvesting must adhere to specific regulatory rules, including those regarding wash sales and the recognition of capital gains.
  • T. Rowe Price recommends that investors consult with a tax professional before pursuing a tax-loss harvesting strategy to navigate complex rules regarding wash sales, loss carryovers, and short- versus long-term gains.
  • Swapping an ETF with another that tracks the same index from a different issuer, such as Vanguard versus Schwab, violates the 'substantially identical' rule regarding wash sales.
  • Wealthfront manages the interaction and timing of trades within its portfolio management service to help avoid wash sales, which occur when a security sold to harvest a loss is re-purchased within 30 days.
  • Investors may need to use alternative ETFs when investing in tax-deferred accounts to avoid wash sales.
b4b4895c canonical: portfolio return (concept) — flat · 2 aliases · 2 embeddings
aliases (2): portfolio return portfolio returns
embeddings (2): portfolio return portfolio returns
8 fact sample(s)
  • The overall portfolio return is calculated by summing the weighted contributions of each asset, which for the specified portfolio results in a 6.8% return (0.032 + 0.015 + 0.021 = 0.068).
  • Return and standard deviation are key parameters for understanding portfolio risk and return because they define the distribution of potential outcomes.
  • Portfolio return is calculated as the weighted average of the returns of individual assets, where weights represent the proportion of each asset in the portfolio.
  • While there is a positive relationship between portfolio return and risk, higher risk does not guarantee a more attractive return.
  • The formula for calculating the return of a portfolio (Rp) is a weighted average of the individual returns of its holdings: Rp = (w1 * R1) + (w2 * R2) + ... + (wn * Rn), where R1, R2, ..., Rn are the individual returns of the holdings and w1, w2, ..., wn are t
32628975 canonical: surplus optimization (concept) — flat · 1 aliases · 1 embeddings
aliases (1): surplus optimization
embeddings (1): surplus optimization
4 fact sample(s)
  • Liability-relative asset allocation is an investment framework that accounts for the liabilities a portfolio is intended to fund, and it includes a specific extension of mean–variance optimization known as surplus optimization.
  • Surplus optimization is an economic balance sheet approach that extends to the liability side of the balance sheet to identify the most efficient asset class mixes when liabilities are present.
  • Approaches to liability-relative asset allocation include surplus optimization, a hedging/return-seeking portfolios approach, and an integrated asset–liability approach.
  • Surplus optimization is an approach to liability-relative asset allocation that involves applying Mean-Variance Optimization (MVO) to surplus returns.
06e417be canonical: real assets (concept) — flat · 1 aliases · 1 embeddings

61c374a3 canonical: JonBenét Ramsey (entity) — flat · 2 aliases · 2 embeddings
aliases (2): JonBenét Ramsey JonBenét Patricia Ramsey

10 fact sample(s)
  • JonBenét Patricia Ramsey was a six-year-old child beauty pageant contestant who was reported missing from her family's home in Boulder, Colorado, on December 26, 1996, and was found dead in the basement later that same day.
  • The Boulder County Coroner's Office determined the cause of death for JonBenét Ramsey was asphyxia by strangulation associated with craniocerebral trauma from blunt force injury to the head.
  • A handwritten ransom note found in the Ramsey home demanded $118,000 for the safe return of JonBenét Ramsey, an amount roughly equivalent to the Christmas bonus received by her father, John Ramsey.
  • Unidentified male DNA recovered from JonBenét Ramsey's clothing and the crime scene did not match family members or known associates, which supported the theory of an intruder rather than family involvement.
  • As of early 2026, the Boulder Police Department continues to treat the killing of JonBenét Ramsey as an active investigation and a top priority.

041fdd1b canonical: BRAF V600E-mutated anaplastic thyroid cancer (concept) — flat · 1 aliases · 1 embeddings
aliases (1): BRAF V600E-mutated anaplastic thyroid cancer
embeddings (1): BRAF V600E-mutated anaplastic thyroid cancer
3 fact sample(s)
  • A Phase II multicenter trial led by Mark Zafereo and Maria Cabanillas increased average progression-free survival for patients with BRAF V600E-mutated anaplastic thyroid cancer from 6.7 months to over one year.
  • The Phase II trial for BRAF V600E-mutated anaplastic thyroid cancer evaluated a pre-surgical combination of the targeted therapies dabrafenib and trametinib plus the immunotherapy drug pembrolizumab.
  • The triple therapy regimen of dabrafenib, trametinib, and pembrolizumab enabled 74% of patients with BRAF V600E-mutated anaplastic thyroid cancer to have their tumor removed surgically, compared to a historical rate of 5%.
edf71ac2 canonical: 2022 Health Information National Trends Survey (concept) — flat · 2 aliases · 2 embeddings
aliases (2): 2022 Health Information National Trends Survey HINTS 2022
embeddings (2): 2022 Health Information National Trends Survey HINTS 2022
3 fact sample(s)
  • A study led by Sanjay Shete, Ph.D., based on data from the 2022 Health Information National Trends Survey, found that only 40% of American adults recognize consuming alcohol as a cancer risk.
  • Nearly 70% of American adults surveyed in the 2022 Health Information National Trends Survey reported that they drink alcoholic beverages.
  • Just over 30% of American adults surveyed in the 2022 Health Information National Trends Survey believe that cancer prevention is not possible.
33f31d32 canonical: RAS-driven apoptosis (concept) — flat · 1 aliases · 1 embeddings
aliases (1): RAS-driven apoptosis
embeddings (1): RAS-driven apoptosis
1 fact sample(s)
  • Pharmacological upregulation of β-catenin and cMYC through GSK-3β inhibition triggers RAS-driven apoptosis in cancer cells and inhibits tumor growth.
a7535f8a canonical: Tazemetostat (concept) — flat · 1 aliases · 1 embeddings
aliases (1): Tazemetostat
embeddings (1): Tazemetostat
2 fact sample(s)
  • The FDA has approved PARP inhibitors, including Nilaparib, Rucaparib, and Tazemetostat, for cancer treatment.
  • The FDA has approved PARP inhibitors including Nilaparib, Rucaparib, and Tazemetostat for cancer treatment.
0513230f canonical: APC mutations (concept) — flat · 1 aliases · 1 embeddings
aliases (1): APC mutations
embeddings (1): APC mutations
2 fact sample(s)
  • Hyperactivation of the WNT/β-catenin pathway can lead to the selective death of colorectal cancer cells with APC mutations.
  • The majority of colorectal cancers (CRCs) exhibit genetic changes in the WNT pathway, such as APC mutations or activating mutations in CTNNB1 (β-catenin), which can encourage initial tumor growth.
1ce6883d canonical: Homologous recombination DNA repair (concept) — flat · 2 aliases · 2 embeddings
aliases (2): Homologous recombination DNA repair HR DNA repair
embeddings (2): Homologous recombination DNA repair HR DNA repair
2 fact sample(s)
  • Deficiency in homologous recombination DNA repair renders cells sensitive to poly(ADP-ribose) polymerase (PARP) inhibition.
  • Deficiency in homologous recombination DNA repair is associated with sensitivity to poly(ADP-ribose) polymerase (PARP) inhibition.
fbcf30b3 canonical: topotecan (concept) — flat · 1 aliases · 1 embeddings
aliases (1): topotecan
embeddings (1): topotecan
2 fact sample(s)
  • A 2018 phase I study published in the Journal of Clinical Oncology by Thomas et al. evaluated the ATR inhibitor M6620 in combination with topotecan in patients with advanced solid tumors.
  • A phase I study conducted by Thomas et al. (2018) evaluated the ATR inhibitor M6620 in combination with topotecan in patients with advanced solid tumors.

  • Of the 28 FDA approvals for drugs announced in the first half of 2025, 12 were for immunotherapy drugs.

  • T cells, also known as T lymphocytes or CD3 cells, are a type of immune cell that can directly attack virus-infected cells or act as regulators of the immune system.

  • A semiconductor diode, also known as a p-n junction diode, is composed of p-type and n-type semiconductor materials.
  • In a p-n junction diode, the p-type semiconductor is doped with boron to create positive charge holes, while the n-type semiconductor is doped with antimony to add extra negative charge electrons.
  • When p-type and n-type semiconductor blocks are joined to form a p-n junction diode, extra electrons from the n-type combine with holes in the p-type to create a depletion zone that lacks free electrons or holes.

reason: no candidates above threshold — new flat big-seed

  • Epistemic justification is considered a positive property of beliefs because justified beliefs originate from reliable processes that promote the goal of believing what is true rather than what is false.
  • Process reliabilism holds that a belief is justified if it is produced by a reliable process type, defined as a process that produces true beliefs more often than false beliefs.
  • In process reliabilism, vision is considered a reliable process type if the beliefs produced by vision are true more often than they are false across all times and places.

#765 predictive homeostasis (6 facts) → genesis → bigseed "predictive homeostasis"
reason: no candidates above threshold — new flat big-seed
incoming fact samples:
  • Predictive homeostasis is defined as the normal range of a mediator, such as glucocorticoids, that occurs in response to reasonably predictable events like circadian changes, reproduction, or migration.
  • Reactive homeostasis is defined as the range of a circulating mediator that exists above predictive homeostasis, up to the level where exposure to that mediator becomes damaging (wear and tear).
  • The reactive scope of blood levels of a mediator spans the ranges of predictive homeostasis and reactive homeostasis, extending from the minimum level required to maintain homeostasis up to the point where mediator levels cause harm.

#768 cybernetic interpretation of homeostasis (16 facts) → genesis → bigseed "cybernetic interpretation of homeostasis"
reason: no candidates above threshold — new flat big-seed
incoming fact samples:
  • In medicine, the cybernetic interpretation of homeostasis characterizes health as a stable physiological state that an organism must preserve or return to following a perturbation.
  • The cybernetic interpretation of homeostasis characterizes the environment primarily as a source of perturbations that an organism must block or compensate for, which limits the understanding of health in relation to the environment.
  • Bechtel and Bich (2024, 2025) argue that the debate over physiological regulation revolves around the cybernetic interpretation of homeostasis, which is conceived as negative feedback to a setpoint.

  • In a closed-loop feedback system, effector activity opposes and buffers against changes in the controlled variable.

  • The winner's curse was originally discovered in the field by engineers at an oil company who noticed that the rights they won to drill in specific spots yielded less oil than their geologists had predicted, because the auctions they won wer
  • Max Bazerman and William Samuelson demonstrated the winner's curse phenomenon in laboratory settings after it had been identified in the field.

  • Sentence structure (syntax) serves as an indicator of tone: longer, complex sentences often indicate a formal, scholarly, or descriptive tone, while shorter, simpler sentences suggest a more direct, informal, or urgent tone.

  • Liability-relative asset allocation is an investment framework that accounts for the liabilities a portfolio is intended to fund, and it includes a specific extension of mean–variance optimization known as surplus optimization.
  • Surplus optimization is an economic balance sheet approach that extends to the liability side of the balance sheet to identify the most efficient asset class mixes when liabilities are present.
  • Approaches to liability-relative asset allocation include surplus optimization, a hedging/return-seeking portfolios approach, and an integrated asset–liability approach.
